source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In figure, let a beam of x rays of wavelength 0.125\,\text{nm} be incident on an NaCl crystal at angle \( heta = 45.0^\circ \) to the top face of the crystal and a family of reflecting planes. Let the reflecting planes have separation \( d = 0.252\,\text{nm} \). The crystal is turned through angle \( \phi \) around an axis perpendicular to the plane of the page until these reflecting planes give diffraction maxima. What is the larger value of \( \phi \) if it is turned counterclockwise?

image_dependency: question_text begins with 'In figure' and refers to the crystal orientation and reflecting planes shown in the figure
the asked quantity is a rotation angle relative to depicted faces/planes, which depends on spatial relationships
multi_step: solving likely requires combining Bragg diffraction condition with the incident-angle/plane geometry
the prompt asks for the 'larger value' of phi when turned counterclockwise, suggesting multiple candidate angles must be considered and compared
question_text contains several coupled quantities (wavelength, incident angle, plane spacing, rotation) rather than a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a specific numeric angle ('37.8^\circ')
choices are provided, enabling straightforward answer checking against option C
the underlying physics setup is formula-based, so the final value can be checked computationally

risk_flags: question_text appears to contain a formatting/OCR artifact in '\( heta = 45.0^\circ \)', which may slightly affect parsing

decision_reasons: image, question text, and answer are all present, so the sample has full core semantic support
initial scoring is strong: high image dependency, high verifiability, and solid multi-step reasoning value
the only noted issue is a minor formatting/OCR artifact, which does not appear to prevent recovery of the problem
